source.multipart_basis: Decomposition derived from recumbent three-wheel HPV + ASME HPVC rollcage practice: two longitudinal main tubes form the RRPS, closed by crossmembers and a helmet-plane cross-brace, with separate seat-back structure, pedal-box bulkhead, steering column support, rear-wheel axle mount, headrest bar, and a ring roll-over bar. Tube + weld / node joint topology is inherent to 4130 chromoly frame construction. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the Roll-over Protection System (RRPS) and integrated chassis of a recumbent, fully-faired three-wheel human-powered vehicle intended for a 24-hour human-powered-vehicle endurance benchmark, fitted for one rider at a time during crew rotations. The RRPS must consist of at least two longitudinal bars running from front bulkhead to rear bulkhead with lateral separation no greater than 200 mm measured at the rider's head position, connected by cross-bracing at the top of the helmet-clearance plane so that the rider's helmet is fully enclosed within a protected cell. Material is 4130 chromoly steel tubing (Young's modulus 205 GPa, Poisson ratio 0.29, yield strength 435 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 670 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3) with minimum outer diameter 25.4 mm and minimum wall thickness 1.6 mm for all RRPS primary tubes. Inside the RRPS, mandatory head-foam lining of thickness at least 25 mm (closed-cell EPP, density 45 kg/m^3) must be provided around the driver's head-impact envelope. The vehicle footprint must remain within 2.5 m length x 0.9 m width x 1.1 m height. Load cases: (LC1) vertical crush load 2670 N (600 lbf) applied to the top of the RRPS at the helmet position while the wheel-axle mounts are fully fixed; (LC2) lateral side load 1330 N (300 lbf) applied at the same top-cage point; (LC3) forward longitudinal load 1330 N (300 lbf) at the top point simulating a roll-over slide; (LC4) 24-hour duty-cycle fatigue representation as a pedaling reaction of 800 N alternating at 90 rpm (1.5 Hz) at the pedal axis for 86,400 cycles. Pass/fail: under LC1-LC3 no point in the RRPS may displace more than 25 mm toward the rider's head envelope and no tube element may exceed 290 MPa von Mises stress (435 / 1.5 ultimate factor); under LC4 the maximum alternating stress at the pedal-boom weld must remain below the 10^5-cycle S-N endurance limit of 4130 taken as 240 MPa; first global torsional natural frequency must exceed 8 Hz to avoid shimmy coupling. Deliverables: STEP AP242 chassis + RRPS geometry, static FEA report for LC1-LC3, fatigue check for LC4, modal analysis of first three torsion/bending modes, and a mass report with the bare RRPS tube mass separated from the rest of the chassis.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This HPV chassis + RRPS is delivered as a welded 4130 tube-frame multi-part assembly mirroring recumbent HPV practice: (A) two longitudinal main RRPS tubes running front-to-rear bulkhead (>= 25.4 mm OD x 1.6 mm wall, <= 200 mm lateral separation at helmet), (B) upper helmet-plane cross-brace tubes enclosing the helmet within a protected cell, (C) a roll-over hoop / main ring behind the seat, (D) seat-back structure tied into the RRPS, (E) pedal-box bulkhead at the front carrying the pedal boom and LC4 alternating load, (F) steering-column support stanchion, (G) rear-wheel axle-mount bracket transferring rear wheel reactions, (H) headrest bar supporting the rider head foam, and (I) EPP closed-cell head foam (>= 25 mm) lining inside the protected cell. FEM verification runs on the assembled model only; per-part stress reported. All inherited load magnitudes (LC1-LC4), the 4130 property set, and R1-R4 pass/fail thresholds are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria (R_asm1 connection DCR, R_asm2 welded-node stress concentration under LC1, R_asm3 pedal-boom weld fatigue class) are added.
short_prompt:
Design the Roll-over Protection System (RRPS) and integrated chassis of a recumbent, fully-faired three-wheel human-powered vehicle for a 24-hour HPV endurance benchmark, delivered as a welded multi-part 4130 tube-frame assembly.

The RRPS uses two longitudinal bars from front to rear bulkhead with lateral separation no greater than 200 mm at the rider's head position, cross-bracing above the helmet, 4130 chromoly tubing (yield 435 MPa, ultimate 670 MPa, E 205 GPa, density 7850 kg/m^3) with minimum OD 25.4 mm x wall 1.6 mm on primary tubes, and mandatory >= 25 mm closed-cell EPP head-foam lining (density 45 kg/m^3). Vehicle footprint stays within 2.5 m length x 0.9 m width x 1.1 m height.

Load cases.
- LC1 vertical crush 2670 N (600 lbf) at the top of the RRPS.
- LC2 lateral side load 1330 N (300 lbf) at the top cage point.
- LC3 forward longitudinal load 1330 N (300 lbf) at the top point.
- LC4 fatigue: pedaling reaction 800 N alternating at 90 rpm (1.5 Hz) for 86,400 cycles.

First global torsional natural frequency must exceed 8 Hz.

prompt.title: 24-hour human-powered vehicle RRPS + chassis (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Welded 4130 chromoly tube-frame HPV chassis (2.5 x 0.9 x 1.1 m): two longitudinal RRPS main tubes (<=200 mm lateral separation at helmet), helmet-plane cross-brace, main ring, seat-back, pedal-box bulkhead, steering-column support, rear-wheel axle mount, headrest bar, and 25 mm EPP head foam. Must pass 2670 N vertical crush, 1330 N lateral, 1330 N longitudinal, LC4 800 N pedaling fatigue, head-envelope intrusion <=25 mm, tube stress <=290 MPa, and torsional mode >=8 Hz at the assembled level.

boundary_conditions.wheel_axle_mounts: fully fixed during LC1-LC3 roll-over load cases
boundary_conditions.pedal_axis: reaction point for alternating 800 N pedaling load (LC4)

objective.primary: pass HPVC RRPS and pedal-boom fatigue compliance

parts.name: longitudinal RRPS main tube
parts.role: longitudinal 4130 chromoly tube running from front bulkhead to rear bulkhead; forms the two primary load-path bars of the RRPS

parts.name: helmet-plane cross-brace tube
parts.role: transverse and diagonal cross-braces at the top of the helmet-clearance plane that enclose the helmet within a protected cell

parts.name: main roll-over ring
parts.role: main hoop / ring behind the seat carrying LC1 vertical crush load into the longitudinal RRPS tubes

parts.name: seat-back structure
parts.role: triangulated 4130 tube structure supporting the recumbent seat back; ties into the main ring and longitudinal tubes

parts.name: pedal-box bulkhead
parts.role: front bulkhead carrying the pedal boom axis and reacting LC4 alternating 800 N pedaling load

parts.name: steering-column support stanchion
parts.role: stanchion supporting the steering column between the pedal-box bulkhead and the seat-back plane

parts.name: rear-wheel axle-mount bracket
parts.role: bracket transferring rear-wheel reactions into the rear of the RRPS; one of the LC1-LC3 fixed constraints

parts.name: headrest bar
parts.role: transverse bar at the top of the seat back supporting rider headrest and head foam

parts.name: EPP head-foam lining
parts.role: closed-cell EPP foam lining (>=25 mm) around the head-impact envelope inside the RRPS protected cell

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: HPV body frame: +X forward, +Y port, +Z up; rider nominal seated position mid-chassis

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all welded tube-node interfaces (longitudinal-to-ring, longitudinal-to-cross-brace, seat-back-to-ring, pedal-box-to-longitudinal) <= 1.0 under governing LC1 2670 N vertical crush.
Welded-node stress concentration under LC1 crush: peak hotspot stress at the intersection of longitudinal tubes and the main ring <= 290 MPa with a weld stress-concentration factor Kt of 2.0 applied.

Pedal-boom weld fatigue class: under LC4 86,400 cycles at 800 N alternating, the pedal-boom-to-pedal-box weld must meet AWS D1.1 Class C (or better) detail, with alternating stress <= 240 MPa confirmed on the assembled weld geometry.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam elements for tubes + solid/shell at welded nodes

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded tube-tube node at helmet plane
interfaces.load_path: helmet-plane cross-brace (B) welded to both longitudinal tubes (A); LC1 vertical crush load applied at helmet top transfers through cross-brace into longitudinals.

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded tube-tube node at rear bulkhead (main ring)
interfaces.load_path: main ring (C) welded to longitudinals (A); governs R_asm2 welded-node stress concentration under LC1.

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded tube-tube nodes on main ring and longitudinals
interfaces.load_path: seat-back structure (D) welded to main ring (C); LC2/LC3 side + longitudinal loads flow through seat back into main ring.

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded tube-plate node at front bulkhead
interfaces.load_path: pedal-box bulkhead (E) welded to front of longitudinal tubes (A); LC4 800 N alternating pedaling load reacts at pedal-boom weld, governing R_asm3 fatigue criterion.
steering-column stanchion (F) welded to pedal-box bulkhead (E); steering reaction enters pedal-box.

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded tube-plate node at rear axle bracket
interfaces.load_path: rear-wheel axle-mount bracket (G) welded to rear of longitudinals (A); provides LC1-LC3 fixed constraint.

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded tube-tube node on main ring
interfaces.load_path: headrest bar (H) welded between longitudinal or main-ring legs; supports head foam (I).

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded / strapped EPP foam to headrest bar
interfaces.load_path: EPP head foam (I) mechanically retained on headrest bar (H) inside the protected cell.

fem_check_policy.rationale: RRPS head-envelope intrusion and welded-node stress are inherent to the tube-node topology: a monolithic model cannot capture weld stress concentration (R_asm2) or pedal-boom fatigue (R_asm3).

notes.why_multi_part: Welded 4130 chromoly tube-frame chassis is a collection of tubes, nodes, and brackets; welded-node stress concentration and pedal-boom weld fatigue class are fundamentally assembly-level phenomena.
notes.speculation_caveats: Tube OD/wall choices beyond the 25.4 x 1.6 mm RRPS floor, ring arc length, and head-foam coverage geometry are programmatic speculation within HPVC practice.
